message:
test(readme): cover Missing Data extras (fd90.213 / br-80q21)

Extends readme_missing_data_handling_compiles_and_runs to cover 5 APIs
documented in the README's Missing Data Handling section (lines 963-988)
that previously had no integration coverage:

- Series.first_valid_index / last_valid_index (line 963)
- DataFrame.first_valid_index / last_valid_index
- df.fillna_method("ffill") / df.fillna_method("bfill") (line 988)
- df.combine_first(&other) (line 982: "Combine two DataFrames, filling
  nulls from `other`")
- df.update(&other) (line 985: "Non-null values in `other` overwrite df")

Found via self-review on the last 3 commits.

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

All 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-80ttb","title":"fd90.154: clarify parity_gate vs lib-test pass semantics in auto-generated table","description":"fd90.74-153 follow-on. The auto-generated table in FEATURE_PARITY.md (added by fd90.153) shows '1 passing / 430 total' as the headline — which is technically what parity_gate_result.json reports, but misleadingly low. Lib-level cargo test -p fp-conformance --lib reports 557 passing / 5 failing. The two metrics measure different things:

  - parity_gate_result.json 'pass': per-packet gate threshold (typically 0 fixture failures required)
  - cargo test --lib pass: individual #[test] functions returning Ok

A user glancing at the table will see '1 passing / 430 total' and conclude the project is mostly broken — but the lib test pass rate is ~99%.

Fix: add a clarifying preamble to the auto-generated block explaining the parity-gate semantics + linking to the lib-level pass rate. The headline aggregate counts stay (they're accurate for what they measure), but framed honestly so readers don't misinterpret.

Pure docs/script change in scripts/gen_feature_parity_table.py + regenerate FEATURE_PARITY.md. No code/behavior change to fp-frame/fp-conformance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:57:00.277404285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:58:38.594519260Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T14:58:38.591824558Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
